review: 1.10.0-RC4d mesh observability (netty.cluster.mesh.* meters) — adversarial design-review before implementation
method: Workflow: 4 lenses (wiring/concurrency, metrics-naming/cardinality/meter-type, spec-fidelity/increment-site/YAGNI, honesty/docs/deferral) -> skeptic-verify each finding -> synthesis. The Review phase completed; the Verify + Synthesis phases died on an account session limit (resets 19:10 Asia/Shanghai). The 16 raw findings (deduped to 9 themes) were therefore adjudicated by the maintainer against the real tree (file:line verified) and folded into spec v2.
outcome: spec v1 -> v2; 1 BLOCKER + 4 MAJOR + 2 MINOR + 2 NIT folded before any code was written.

findings_folded.title: BL6 setter-swap runs AFTER broker.start()
findings_folded.verified: NettyWebSocketClusterConfigure.java:402 (broker.start() is inside the broker @Bean, before the sender bean @857; ClusterMessageSender.start() never calls broker.start()).
findings_folded.fold: Redesigned BL6: the meter binder reads the BROKER's OWN ClusterRuntimeStats (mesh.runtimeStats()) for the mesh block — no sharing, no swap, no volatile, no start-ordering hazard. The broker's instance was never orphaned, only unread. Counters partition cleanly by owner (sender = routing/app counters; broker = transport counters), so no unification is needed.

findings_folded.title: IdleCloseHandler is static — cannot reach instance stats; incMeshIdleReaps won't compile
findings_folded.fold: Pass the (final) stats to IdleCloseHandler's constructor; increment in the IdleStateEvent branch before ctx.close().

findings_folded.title: peers.known described as 'advertised ∩ live' but peerSnapshot is the RAW directory read
findings_folded.verified: MeshBroker.java:230 (this.peerSnapshot = peers from directory.peers()); dead-node subtraction @238-252 is local to the tick's reachability count, not persisted.
findings_folded.fold: Corrected the gauge/accessor docs: raw directory snapshot by address TTL; may briefly include a heartbeat-dead peer.

findings_folded.title: A backoff-skipped send would increment BOTH reconnect.backoff_skips AND send.failures
findings_folded.verified: MeshBroker.java:454 (connectionForSend returns null) -> :413 (sendTo counted null as a failure).
findings_folded.fold: Moved reason-specific accounting into connectionForSend (its only caller is sendTo): backoff-skip -> ONLY backoffSkips; genuine dial-fail -> ONLY sendFailures. sendTo no longer blanket-counts null. The four send buckets (skip / failure / backpressure-drop / sent) are now disjoint.

findings_folded.title: Deferring the fan-out reduction gauge over-claimed it needed new sampling + a design pass
findings_folded.verified: MeshBroker.java:303 (interested set already computed on publish); ClusterRuntimeStats room sampler + NettyClusterMeterBinder room gauge already exist as a proven precedent.
findings_folded.fold: Included netty.cluster.mesh.fanout.target_nodes in RC4d, reusing the room sampler pattern (recordMeshFanoutTargets sampler + getMeshFanoutTargetsAvg). It is the single most valuable mesh meter (shows RC4b interest routing working). 9 meters total.

findings_folded.title: frames.sent at enqueue overlaps send.failures on async write-fail
findings_folded.fold: Count frames.sent in the writeAndFlush listener SUCCESS branch (f.isSuccess()), disjoint from the async-failure and backpressure-drop paths.

findings_folded.title: connections.active <= peers.known is not a strict invariant
findings_folded.verified: outbound channels can linger (until idle-reap/close) for a peer dropped from the snapshot.
findings_folded.fold: Softened the doc wording to 'usually <= peers.known'; a transient overshoot is normal.

findings_folded.title: the known-vs-active gap is also structural under interest routing
findings_folded.fold: Added a clause: broadcasts dial only the interested subset, so a large gap can mean a quiet topology OR effective interest pruning.

findings_folded.title: spec grounding table mislabeled two existing meshSendFailures sites
findings_folded.verified: MeshBroker.java:431 increments backpressure (not failures); accurate failure sites are 413/418/437.

alsoResolved.note: Moot under the v2 BL6 redesign — stats stays final (no swap), so there is no read-during-swap window.

verdict.note: v2 folds all 9 findings with fixes verified against the tree. The re-review (round 2) was deferred due to the account session limit; the adversarial gate is carried by (a) this grounded adjudication and (b) the post-implementation adversarial impl-review. Implementation is per-task TDD with per-task commits on feature/1.10.0-mesh-rc4d.
